ZCAM SDK
ZCAM SDK
    Ask in ChatGPT

    ZCAM vs Other Approaches

    Numerous technologies exist for image authenticity and provenance. These solutions address different problem spaces and offer varying trade-offs.

    ZCAM provides a complete mobile-first solution for photo authenticity: hardware-backed attestation, optional zero-knowledge proofs, embedded verification data, and a simple verified/not-verified output tailored to human-captured zPhotos.

    Below is a feature comparison showing what each approach provides. See the sections below for more detail.

    Comparison Table

    ApproachHardware AttestationZK ProofsEmbedded VerificationUser-friendly OutputCamera PhotosUse Case
    ZCAM✅✅✅✅✅Complete mobile photo authenticity solution
    C2PA (generic)❌❌✅❌✅Edit history tracking and provenance
    Physical Camera❓❌✅❌✅Some cameras support hardware-backed signing; support varies
    JPEG Trust❌❌✅✅✅Trust evaluation framework (leverages C2PA)
    SynthID❌❌✅❌❌AI watermarking and detection
    AI Content Marking❌❌✅❌❌AI-generated content marking (watermarking or C2PA)

    Other tooling

    C2PA

    C2PA is a content credential standard that creates a verifiable history for images.

    It is not specific to "authentic" photos: C2PA can be applied to camera captures and AI-generated images alike. A C2PA manifest records origin and edit history; each manifest is signed using public-key cryptography.

    Key differences from ZCAM:
    • C2PA doesn't require hardware attestation; any signing key can be used.
    • C2PA focuses on edit history, while ZCAM focuses on capture authenticity.
    • ZCAM uses C2PA as the container format but adds hardware-backed guarantees.
    • C2PA is a general-purpose tool for attaching historical metadata; ZCAM focuses on proving the authenticity of an image.

    More technical information is available in the How it Works section under C2PA.

    JPEG Trust

    JPEG Trust provides a framework for evaluating an image's trustworthiness. It leverages C2PA manifests to build trust profiles and evaluators that help determine authenticity.

    Key differences from ZCAM:
    • JPEG Trust is an evaluation framework, not a capture/verification system.
    • It can consume ZCAM-generated C2PA manifests.
    • Focuses on trust scoring rather than raw cryptographic verification.

    SynthID

    SynthID (Google) is an invisible watermark framework that embeds resilient data into images. JUMBF metadata can be stripped or altered by edits; SynthID uses ML-based embeddings that a reader model can detect.

    Key differences from ZCAM:
    • SynthID focuses on AI-detection; ZCAM focuses on authenticity verification.
    • ZCAM provides cryptographic guarantees; SynthID provides probabilistic detection.

    IPTC Photo Metadata

    IPTC Photo Metadata defines standardized fields and labels for image metadata. It is a metadata format rather than a verification system.

    Key differences from ZCAM:
    • IPTC is a metadata format, not a verification system.
    • Can be used alongside ZCAM for standardized metadata fields.
    • No cryptographic guarantees or hardware attestation.

    AI Content Marking

    Several companies provide tools for marking AI-generated content:

    • Meta Watermarking: Durable watermarks for AI-generated content that aim to survive cropping and edits.
    • OpenAI C2PA: OpenAI-generated images include C2PA manifests indicating the generation source.
    Key differences from ZCAM:
    • These solutions mark AI-generated content; ZCAM verifies authentic human-captured zPhotos.
    • They lack hardware attestation.
    • Different goals: provenance/marking for AI content vs. cryptographic authenticity verification for human-captured zPhotos.

    When to Use ZCAM

    ZCAM is the right choice when you need:

    • Hardware-backed authenticity: Guarantees that zPhotos were taken on a specific device
    • Cryptographic verification: Strong cryptographic proofs versus probabilistic detection
    • Mobile-first: Designed for iOS (Android support planned)
    • Simple verification: Easy API that returns verified/not-verified without requiring manifest parsing
    • End-to-end solution: From capture to verification with optional ZK proof integration

    Consider other approaches when:

    • You need to mark AI-generated content (use SynthID or AI Content Marking)
    • You want a general-purpose edit history system (use generic C2PA)
    • You need watermarking resilient to heavy editing (use SynthID)
    • You're working with non-mobile platforms (use C2PA or other standards)
